description: Figure 9 presents quantitative strengthening mechanism analyses for a Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel processed at hot-rolling reductions of 45 % (HR45), 68 % (HR68), and 82 % (HR82). Panel (a) plots the true flow stress at yielding σ_F0—defined as σ_y minus precipitate strengthening σ_p and dislocation strengthening σ_ds—versus the inverse square root of the mean effective grain size d_e^(-1/2). The red Hall-Petch fitting line gives σ_F0 = 203.1 + 733.4 d_e^(-1/2) (MPa), yielding a friction stress of 203.1 MPa and a Hall-Petch coefficient of 733.4 MPa·μm^(1/2). The three visible data points correspond to effective grain sizes of 21.82 μm (359.5 MPa), 11.55 μm (420.0 MPa), and 7.02 μm (479.3 MPa). Panel (b) is a stacked bar chart illustrating the calculated contributions of σ_F (friction stress), σ_p-shearing, σ_p-Orowan, σ_ds (dislocation strengthening), and σ_gb (grain boundary strengthening) to the yield strength for each rolling state, with white triangles marking experimentally measured values (σ_exp). The figure shows that grain boundary strengthening is the predominant contributor and increases markedly with rolling reduction, while dislocation strengthening also rises; together they account for the yield strength increment. Caption states that the calculated results show good consistency with experimental data, validating the strengthening analysis.
